title: Library Checkout Refactor
description:
Refactor and extend a small library checkout system. The goal is not to build every feature a real library might need; the goal is to make clear object boundaries, protect invariants, and justify design choices.

You will model library items, members, checkout rules, and overdue fines. Start from a deliberately simple design, then improve it using encapsulation, composition, and polymorphism where those choices reduce coupling.
requirements: Model Book, Magazine, and DVD items with a shared public interface for title, item_id, availability, checkout duration, and daily fine
Create a Member class that protects borrowed items from direct external mutation
Create Regular and Premium borrowing policies using composition rather than hard-coded conditionals
Create a Library class that checks out and returns items while enforcing availability and borrowing limits
Use polymorphism so due dates and fines do not require type-check chains in Library
Use one appropriate creation helper or factory only if it simplifies loading item data
Write behavioral tests for checkout, return, borrowing limits, defensive copies, and overdue fine calculation
Include a short DESIGN.md section explaining one inheritance/composition choice and one rejected over-engineering choice
Keep the command-line or demo interface minimal: enough to demonstrate the public API, not a full application shell

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Classes are mostly data bags or one large manager; responsibilities are unclear

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic classes exist, but some responsibilities or relationships are misplaced

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Classes have clear responsibilities, public APIs are usable, and internal state is protected

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: The model is cohesive, minimal, and easy to extend without exposing unnecessary internals

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Design relies on repeated type checks or inheritance used only for code reuse

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Some polymorphism or composition is present, but conditionals still drive core behavior

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Variable behavior is delegated to objects with clear interfaces

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Design choices are simple, substitutable, and well justified

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Few or no meaningful tests; changes are hard to verify

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic tests cover happy paths, with limited edge-case or invariant coverage

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Tests check public behavior, important edge cases, and collaboration between objects

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Tests make refactoring safe and avoid depending on private implementation details

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: No useful explanation of design decisions

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Explains what was built, but not why key choices were made

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Clearly justifies major class relationships and rejected alternatives

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Shows mature judgment about simplicity, coupling, and future change

title: Text Tool Plugin API
description:
Design a small text-processing tool with a plugin API. This project focuses on extensibility through interfaces and dependency injection, not on loading arbitrary external code.

The core application should be stable while new commands can be added as separate plugin classes. Students should favor a small, well-tested API over a large framework.
requirements: Create a Document class that protects text content behind a small public API
Define a Plugin interface or Protocol with name and execute(document) behavior
Implement at least three plugins: WordCount, UpperCase, and FindReplace
Create a PluginManager that registers plugins and runs a plugin by name
Use dependency injection for any output/logging behavior so tests can use fakes
Add undo for at least one mutating plugin using a simple command object or explicit history object
Avoid Singleton for configuration; pass configuration or options explicitly
Write behavioral tests for plugin registration, execution, unknown plugins, mutation, and undo
Include a short DESIGN.md section explaining the plugin contract and how a fourth plugin would be added

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: New plugins require broad changes to core code

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Plugins work, but the contract is unclear or coupled to concrete implementations

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: New plugins can be added through a clear, documented interface

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: The API is small, expressive, and easy to test without special framework machinery

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Patterns are absent where needed or added as unnecessary boilerplate

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Uses one or two patterns, but the design benefit is only partly clear

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Uses composition, command/history, or factory-like creation only where it simplifies change

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: Pattern choices are restrained, well-integrated, and explicitly justified

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Tests are missing or mostly assert private implementation details

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Basic plugin behavior is tested, but edge cases and collaboration are thin

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Tests cover public behavior, plugin interaction, fakes, and error cases

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: The test suite supports confident refactoring of the plugin architecture

levels.label: Insufficient
levels.description: Code is hard to follow and the plugin API is undocumented

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Code works but naming, organization, or API documentation needs improvement

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Code is organized, readable, and the extension points are documented

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.description: The implementation is small, clear, and demonstrates professional OOP style
